name: Runtime State Primitives
description: Build the runtime-state subsystems the Runner will later plug into: persistent handoffs, the Mustache-ish template engine, context injection, run state machine, cost metrics, provider registry, and a test-oriented MockProvider. Everything here writes or reads artifacts on disk atomically and is safe to unit-test in isolation.

waves.name: HandoffStore class
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/handoffs.ts` per §4.5.1. Class `HandoffStore` constructor(runDir: string). Methods: `write(id, value, schema?)`, `read<T>(id, schema?)`, `exists(id)`, `list()`. Storage: `<runDir>/handoffs/<id>.json` pretty-printed. Writes use atomicWriteJson (task_7). Validates value against optional Zod schema BEFORE writing — throw HandoffSchemaError(id, issues) on failure. Reads validate against optional schema and throw HandoffSchemaError on mismatch. `list()` returns ids (strip .json extension) sorted alphabetically. Re-export from `src/index.ts`.

waves.name: Mustache-style template renderer
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/template.ts` exporting `renderTemplate(tpl: string, vars: Record<string,unknown>): string`. Support ONLY the syntax documented in §4.5.2: `{{name}}`, `{{name.path.to.field}}`, `{{name[i].field}}`, and `{{#each name}}...{{/each}}`. No helpers, no partials, no conditionals. Missing path resolves to empty string (log a debug warning). `{{#each arr}}` iterates arrays; inside the block `{{.}}` is the current item, `{{field}}` is the field of the current item. Throw `FlowDefinitionError` on unbalanced `{{#each}}`/`{{/each}}` pairs. This is a pure function — no I/O. Reference §4.5.2 explicitly in a doc comment.

waves.name: StateMachine and state.json persistence
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/state.ts` per §4.8. Class `StateMachine` constructor(runDir, flowName, flowVersion, runId, input). Initializes RunState with all steps pending. Methods: `init(steps: string[])`, `startStep(id)`, `completeStep(id, artifacts?, handoffs?)`, `failStep(id, errorMessage)`, `skipStep(id)`, `markRun(status)`, `load(): Promise<RunState>`, `save(): Promise<void>`. `save()` uses atomicWriteJson to `<runDir>/state.json` after every mutation. Also export `loadState(runDir)` and `verifyCompatibility(state, flowName, flowVersion): void` (throws PipelineError on version mismatch per §4.8.2 step 2). Re-export from `src/index.ts`.

waves.name: CostTracker and metrics.json
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/cost.ts` per §4.7. Export `StepMetrics` type and `CostTracker` class constructor(runDir). Methods: `record(metrics: StepMetrics): Promise<void>` — appends to in-memory list AND rewrites `<runDir>/metrics.json` via atomicWriteJson (as full array, not ndjson, for easier reading). `summary(): { totalUsd: number; totalTokens: number; perStep: StepMetrics[] }`. `load(): Promise<void>` reads back metrics.json if it exists (for resume). The costUsd is the API-equivalent estimate — add a TODO comment referencing the subscription caveat in §4.7. Re-export from index.ts.

waves.name: ProviderRegistry
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/providers/registry.ts` per §4.6.5. Class `ProviderRegistry`: `register(p: Provider)` (throws if name collides with existing), `get(name): Provider` (throws FlowDefinitionError 'unknown provider' if missing), `has(name)`, `list(): readonly Provider[]`. Also export a `defaultRegistry` singleton that is initially EMPTY — the ClaudeProvider is registered into it by a later task (task_30). Re-export class + singleton from `src/index.ts`.

waves.name: MockProvider for tests
waves.description: Create `packages/core/src/testing/mock-provider.ts` per §4.6.9 closing paragraph. Class `MockProvider implements Provider` with name:'mock' and configurable capabilities (all true by default, models: ['mock-model']). Constructor accepts `{ responses: Record<string, InvocationResponse | (req, ctx) => InvocationResponse>, capabilities?: Partial<ProviderCapabilities> }`. `invoke()` looks up response by `ctx.stepId` (exact match) or throws a helpful Error naming the stepId. `authenticate()` returns { ok:true, billingSource:'local', detail:'mock provider' }. Optional `stream()` yields a single text.delta + usage + turn.end. Re-export from `src/testing/index.ts` (subpath export from task_2).

waves.name: Context injection assembly
waves.description:
Create `packages/core/src/context-inject.ts` exporting `assemblePrompt({ promptBody, handoffs, inputVars, stepVars }): string`. Implementation: (1) run renderTemplate on `promptBody` with merged vars = { input: inputVars, ...allHandoffs, ...stepVars } so `{{inventory.packages}}` and `{{input.audience}}` both resolve; (2) prepend `<context name="<handoffId>">
<pretty-JSON>
</context>

` for each handoff listed in the step's `contextFrom` array; (3) wrap the rendered body in `<prompt>
...
</prompt>` per §4.5.2 example. Also export `loadHandoffValues(store: HandoffStore, ids: string[]): Promise<Record<string,unknown>>`. Re-export from `src/index.ts`.
